patches.find: (b) exceeds the dome's own 30% falsification threshold,
patches.replace: (b) approaches the dome's own >50% falsification threshold (which requires >50% error for 3 consecutive years — a criterion so generous that nearly-half-wrong predictions still pass),
patches.rationale: CRITICAL: 30% is a fabricated threshold not found anywhere in the dome's published material. The correct threshold is >50% for 3 consecutive years (confirmed raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 65). 39.9% does NOT exceed 50%, making the current verdict factually wrong. TLDR and body both correctly say 50%; this fixes the inconsistency in the verdict paragraph. Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json.

patches.find: The dome's own tracking shows failure. The dome's own tracking shows substantial error.
patches.replace: The dome's own tracking shows substantial error.
patches.rationale: MAJOR: Build/merge artifact from EXP-082 integration. The first sentence ('shows failure') contradicts the body text which explains the dome says PASS at 39.9%. Delete the stutter to leave only the accurate 'shows substantial error' framing. Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json.

patches.find: one (NMP drift rate) self-reports as failing by its own 39.9% error metric
patches.replace: one (NMP drift rate) self-reports as passing at 39.9% error — though only by widening the tolerance to 50% after predictions missed
patches.rationale: MODERATE: The dome does NOT say this test is failing — the dome says PASS (39.9% within 50% tolerance). The summary table correctly shows 'PASS (39.9%)'. Opening prose contradicts the table, inviting a dome defender to point out the inconsistency. Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json.

patches.find: exactly as an azimuthal equidistant projection
patches.replace: analogously to how an azimuthal equidistant projection
patches.rationale: MODERATE: The H(r)≈AE projection correspondence is approximate (ratio 0.96–1.94), good in 30°–60° band but diverging at extremes. 'Exactly as' overstates the correspondence and creates an unnecessary vulnerability. The qualitative argument (the coordinate system IS an AE projection of spherical geometry) remains valid with the softer phrasing. Source: SEC-1.8-post-EXP129.c2.json.
